---
title: "Computer-Aided Leaf Disease Classification Based on Logistic Regression and Transfer Learning Approach"

author:
  - Trishna Saikia
  - Malho Hansdah
  - Koushlendra Kumar Singh

date: "2023-01-01"

slug: leaf-disease-transfer-learning

categories:
  - research
  - plant disease detection
  - agriculture AI
  - transfer learning
  - logistic regression
  - computer vision

description: "Machine Vision and Augmented Intelligence"

summary: "A computer-aided plant leaf disease classification framework combining transfer learning and logistic regression for automated agricultural disease diagnosis."

subtitle: "Transfer learning and logistic regression for plant leaf disease classification."

image: featured.jpg

links:
  - icon: file-richtext-fill
    name: Publication
    url: https://doi.org/10.1007/978-981-99-0189-0_33

  - icon: link-45deg
    name: Springer
    url: https://link.springer.com/chapter/10.1007/978-981-99-0189-0_33
---

## Overview

This work presents a computer-aided framework for automatic plant leaf disease classification using a combination of **transfer learning** and **logistic regression**. The proposed system leverages deep feature representations learned from pre-trained convolutional neural networks and employs a lightweight classification model for efficient disease recognition.

The work was published as a book chapter in **Machine Vision and Augmented Intelligence**, Lecture Notes in Electrical Engineering, Springer, 2023.

## Motivation

Plant diseases significantly impact agricultural productivity and food security worldwide. Early and accurate identification of leaf diseases can help farmers take timely corrective measures and reduce crop losses.

Traditional manual inspection methods are labor-intensive and often require expert knowledge. Advances in computer vision and deep learning have created opportunities for automated plant disease diagnosis systems that can support precision agriculture and smart farming. Transfer learning is particularly useful in such applications because it allows robust feature extraction even when limited labeled agricultural datasets are available.

## Method

The proposed framework consists of two major stages:

### 1. Transfer Learning-Based Feature Extraction

A pre-trained deep convolutional neural network is used to extract discriminative visual features from leaf images. Transfer learning enables the model to utilize knowledge learned from large-scale image datasets and adapt it to agricultural disease classification tasks.

### 2. Logistic Regression Classification

The extracted deep features are provided to a logistic regression classifier that predicts the disease category. Logistic regression offers a simple and computationally efficient alternative while benefiting from the rich feature representations generated by the deep network.

## Key Contributions

* Developed an automated framework for plant leaf disease classification.
* Combined transfer learning and logistic regression for efficient disease recognition.
* Reduced dependence on handcrafted image features.
* Demonstrated the usefulness of deep feature representations in agricultural applications.
* Contributed to AI-assisted disease monitoring for smart agriculture.

## Experimental Evaluation

The framework was evaluated on plant leaf image datasets containing healthy and diseased leaves. Experimental results demonstrated that transfer learning-based representations significantly improved classification performance compared to conventional feature-engineering approaches.

The study showed that combining deep feature extraction with lightweight classifiers can achieve strong performance while maintaining computational efficiency.

## Research Significance

This work contributes to the growing field of **AI for Agriculture** by exploring how computer vision and transfer learning can be applied to plant disease diagnosis. Automated disease detection systems can assist farmers in monitoring crop health, reducing yield losses, and improving agricultural sustainability.

The project also strengthened my experience in computer vision, image classification, and transfer learning before transitioning into medical imaging and physiological computing research.

## Publication

**Trishna Saikia**, **Malho Hansdah**, and **Koushlendra Kumar Singh**.

*Computer-Aided Leaf Disease Classification Based on Logistic Regression and Transfer Learning Approach.*

In: **Machine Vision and Augmented Intelligence**, Lecture Notes in Electrical Engineering, Springer Nature Singapore, 2023.

DOI: https://doi.org/10.1007/978-981-99-0189-0_33
